title: Artificial intelligence drives investments and technological developments
summary: Companies like Amazon, BlackRock and sovereign wealth funds increase their bet on AI, while Anthropic deploys NVIDIA chips on Azure and the debate on open source grows.

title: Corporate restructuring and executive changes in several multinationals
summary: Boeing begins union negotiations, BP replaces its deputy director, Schroders sells its financial division and KNDS seeks backing for its IPO, among other moves.

title: European stock markets close higher and Ibex 35 leads semester gains
summary: The Dax approaches 25,000 points and the Ibex 35 adds more than 12% in the first half, reflecting optimism in European markets.

title: Wall Street closes its best quarter since 2020 with strong gains
summary: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq are heading for their best quarter in years, driven by a broad rally despite tensions with Iran and sector rotation.

title: The yen falls to its lowest level in 40 years against the dollar
summary: The Japanese currency depreciates to 162 yen per dollar, with expectations it will reach 160 by the end of 2026, according to analysts.

title: Key decisions in the US: Supreme Court and telecommunications regulator
summary: The Supreme Court ratifies birthright citizenship, allows exclusion of trans athletes and protects the Fed governor, while the FCC bans devices from sanctioned companies.

title: Commodities: gold suffers its worst quarter and oil faces oversupply
summary: Gold falls on the end of retail euphoria, battery metals futures surge and Morgan Stanley warns of a wave of crude.

title: Bond market moves: European yields and spreads
summary: European bond yields stabilize, spreads narrow and Allianz GI advises short-term bonds.

title: NASA and the space sector: lunar contracts, SpaceX's entry into indices and satellite program in Japan
summary: NASA selects Astrobotic, Firefly and Intuitive Machines for the lunar base; SpaceX joins indices with gains; AST SpaceMobile wins a $1 billion contract in Japan.

title: Cryptocurrencies: Binance sued, Bitcoin falls and UK eases rules
summary: Binance and its founder are sued in London, Bitcoin falls below $60,000 and the British regulator relaxes crypto rules.

title: SanDisk shares surge and lead gains on Wall Street
summary: SanDisk stages a strong rally, with increases above 9%, standing out among the day's most profitable stocks.

title: US job openings rise in May to 7.59 million
summary: The release of job openings data exceeds expectations, causing moves in Treasury bonds.

title: Caterpillar rises strongly after being included in the Russell Top 50
summary: Caterpillar's stock gains value and increases its dividend, standing out as one of the best in the Dow Jones.

title: Fed in the spotlight: White House warning and new communication strategy
summary: The US government warns that the rate hike could harm housing, while experts debate the Fed's 'say less' era.

title: Philip Morris launches new products: cheaper IQOS and Zyn authorized as less harmful
summary: The FDA allows Zyn to be marketed as less harmful than cigarettes, while the company launches a cheaper IQOS model in Japan.

title: Warren Buffett suspends donation to Gates foundation and advises to stay calm
summary: The investor pauses his multi-million dollar donation over the Epstein case, while offering advice on how to act in market crashes.

title: Inflation in Germany and France falls more than expected, ECB remains cautious
summary: The German inflation rate drops significantly in June and the French rate falls to 2%, while ECB members warn it is still high.

title: Corporate moves in Spain: buybacks, dividends and capital increases
summary: ArcelorMittal starts buyback, Inditex ends its own, Ercros approves its delisting, Acciona announces dividend, Oryzon raises capital and OHLA presents forecasts.

title: Notable options moves on Tuesday on Wall Street
summary: Investors show interest in options on GME, AAPL, META, COIN, MSFT and other major tech stocks.

title: Biggest falls in Wall Street indices: Stratergy, Zimmer Biomet and P&G
summary: The stocks that fall the most in the Nasdaq, S&P 500 and Dow Jones register significant losses, led by Stratergy, Zimmer Biomet and P&G.
